---
title: "QC Report"
author: "Your Name"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  md_document:
    variant: mediawiki
params:
  wf_name: "Test Workflow"
  wf_version: "1.0"
  wf_url: "https://example.com/workflow"
  wf_user: "Your Name"
  wf_revision: "abc123"
  nf_version: "This is a test workflow."
  run_name: "Test Run"
  multiqc_json: ""
  multiqc_url: ""
  trackhub_session_url: ""
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, error = FALSE, results = "asis")
options(
  knitr.kable.NA = "",
  big.mark = ","
)
library(knitr)
library(rmarkdown)
library(tidyverse)
library(jsonlite)
```

```{r load_data}

# Load the MultiQC data
multiqc_data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(params$multiqc_json)

data_cols <-
	list(
	  fastqc_cols =
      c(
          "Sample ID" = "Sample ID",
          "Total Seq" = "Total Sequences",
          "Seq Length" = "Sequence length",
          "Median Seq Length" = "median_sequence_length",
          "Basic Stats" = "basic_statistics",
          "Per Base Seq Quality" = "per_base_sequence_quality",
          "Per Tile Seq Quality" = "per_tile_sequence_quality",
          "Per Seq Quality Score" = "per_sequence_quality_scores",
          "Per Base Seq Content" = "per_base_sequence_content",
          "Per Seq GC Content" = "per_sequence_gc_content",
          "Per Base N Content" = "per_base_n_content"
      ),
	  bowtie2 =
	    c(
	      "Total Reads" = "total_reads",
	      "Paired Total" = "paired_total",
	      "Overall Alignment Rate" = "overall_alignment_rate",
	      "Unique Aligned" = "paired_aligned_one",
	      "Multi Aligned" = "paired_aligned_multi"
	    ),
	  gen_stats =
	    c(
	      "Sample ID" = "Sample ID",
        "Raw Total Seq" = "fastqc_raw-total_sequences",
        "Adapter %" = "fastp-pct_adapter",
        "Overall Alignment %" = "bowtie_2_hisat2-overall_alignment_rate",
        "Total Mapped Fragments" = "samtools_flagstat-mapped_passed",
        "Duplication %" = "sambamba_markdup-duplicate_rate",
        "Median Insert Size" = "picard_insertsizemetrics-summed_median",
        "TSS Enrichment" = "atacseqqc-score",
        "NRF" = "encode_library_complexity-nrf",
        "PBC1" = "encode_library_complexity-pbc1",
        "PBC2" = "encode_library_complexity-pbc2",
        "Best Species %" = "kraken-pct_top_one",
        "Unclassified %" = "kraken-pct_unclassified",
        "Raw GC %" = "fastqc_raw-percent_gc",
        "Raw Median Seq Length" = "fastqc_raw-median_sequence_length",
        "Raw FASTQC fail %" = "fastqc_raw-percent_fails",
        "Trimmed GC %" = "fastqc_trimmed-percent_gc",
        "Trimmed Median Seq Length" = "fastqc_trimmed-median_sequence_length",
        "Trimmed FASTQC fail %" = "fastqc_trimmed-percent_fails",
        "Trimmed Total Seq" = "fastqc_trimmed-total_sequences"
	    ),
	  tsse = 
	    c(
	      "Sample ID" = "Sample ID",
	      "Score" = "score"
	    ),
	  homer_fmg = 
	    c(
	      "Sample ID" = "Sample ID",
        "Motif Name" = "motif_name",
        "Consensus" = "consensus",
        #"P-value" = "p_value",
        "log10 P-value" = "log10_p_value",
        "Q value" = "q_value",
        "n of target seq with motif" = "n_of_target_sequences_with_motif",
	      "% of target seq with motif" = "pct_of_target_sequences_with_motif",
	      "n of background seq with motif" = "n_of_background_sequences_with_motif",
	      "% of background seq with motif" = "pct_of_background_sequences_with_motif"
	    )
	)

mqc_to_tbl <- function(mqc,.id = "Sample ID", columns = c()){
  df <- bind_rows(mqc,.id = .id)
  if(length(columns)){
    df <- dplyr::select(df,any_of(columns))
  }
  df
}
parse_fastp <- 
  function(mqc,.id = "Sample ID"){
    sample_ids <- names(mqc)
    map(
      mqc,
      function(x){
        lst(
          reads_before_filtering = x$summary$before_filtering$total_reads,
          reads_after_filtering = x$summary$after_filtering$total_reads,
          trimmed_reads = x$adapter_cutting$adapter_trimmed_reads,
          trimmed_bases = x$adapter_cutting$adapter_trimmed_bases
        )
      }
    ) |>
      bind_rows(.id = .id)
  }




mod_data <-
  list(
    gen_stats = 
      {
        mqc_to_tbl(multiqc_data$report_saved_raw_data$multiqc_general_stats,columns = data_cols$gen_stats) |>
        dplyr::filter( str_detect(`Sample ID`,"\\sR[0-9]$",negate = T) )
      },
    fastqc_raw = mqc_to_tbl(multiqc_data$report_saved_raw_data$multiqc_fastqc_fastqc_raw,columns = data_cols$fastqc_cols),
    fastqc_trimmed = mqc_to_tbl(multiqc_data$report_saved_raw_data$multiqc_fastqc_fastqc_trimmed,columns = data_cols$fastqc_cols),
    fastp = parse_fastp(multiqc_data$report_saved_raw_data$multiqc_fastp),
    bowtie2 = mqc_to_tbl(multiqc_data$report_saved_raw_data$multiqc_bowtie2,columns = data_cols$bowtie2),
    idr_repro = mqc_to_tbl(multiqc_data$report_saved_raw_data$multiqc_encode_idr_reproducibility),
    overlap_repro = mqc_to_tbl(multiqc_data$report_saved_raw_data$multiqc_encode_overlap_reproducibility),
    tsse = mqc_to_tbl(multiqc_data$report_saved_raw_data$multiqc_tss_enrichment_score,columns = data_cols$tsse),
    homer_fmg = mqc_to_tbl(multiqc_data$report_saved_raw_data$multiqc_homer_findmotifsgenome, columns = data_cols$homer_fmg)
    
  )
#mqc_to_tbl(multiqc_data$report_saved_raw_data$)



```


```{r render_workflow_info}

cat("## Run Information\n")

tribble(
	~Workflow, ~ Information,
	"Name", params$wf_name,
	"Version", params$wf_version,
  "Revision", params$wf_revision,
	"URL", params$wf_url,
	"Nextflow Version", params$nf_version,
	"Run Name", params$run_name,
  "Submitted By", params$wf_user,
	"Run Date", as.character(Sys.Date()),
	"MultiQC", params$multiqc_url,
	"Trackhub Session", params$trackhub_session_url
) |>
	kable(
		caption = "Workflow Information",
		col.names = c("Workflow", "Information"),
		align = c("l", "l"),
		table.attr = "class='wikitable sortable'"
	)

```

## Highlights / Overall summary



## Summary Stats

The values in this table represent key metrics that are used to determine sample quality.
These are derived from many of the tools used in the workflow and may have detailed sections.

For the definition of the column, please click the "Definitions" tab.

```{r gen_stats}

cat(
  "<tabber>",
  "|-| Summary =\n"
)

mod_data$gen_stats |>
  mutate(
    across(
      ends_with("%"),
      ~ifelse(.x > 1, .x / 100, .x)
    ),
    across(
      ends_with("%"),
      ~scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.01,big.mark = ",")(.x)
    ),
    across(
      matches("(Raw|Trimmed) Total Seq|Total Mapped Fragments"),
      ~paste(round(.x,3),"M",sep = " ")
    )
  ) |>
  kable(digits = 3,caption = "General statistics")



cat("\n|-| Definitions =\n")
tribble(
  ~Metric,~Tool,~Description,
  "Raw Total Seq","FASTQC","Total number of sequences in the fastq file(s). If paired-end, this will be the sum of R1 and R2.",
  "Adapter %","FASTP","Percentage of reads containing an adapter.",
  "Overall Alignment %", "Bowtie2", "The overall alignment rate of the reads",
  "Total Mapped Fragments", "Bowtie2", "Total number of fragments mapped to the genome.",
  "Duplication %","Sambamba", "The percentage of aligned reads determined to be technical duplicates.",
  "Median Insert Size","Picard","The median insert size of the fragments. This would be calculated as the beginning of R1 to the end of R2.",
  "TSS Enrichment","ENCODE","The signal to noise ratio surrounding TSS sites.",
  "NRF","ENCODE","Non-redundant fraction. Calculated as: total distinct reads / total reads",
  "PBC1","ENCODE","PCR Bottlenecking coefficient 1. Calculated as:  number of genomic positions with only one read / number of distinct genomic positions",
  "PBC2","ENCODE","PCR Bottlenecking coefficient 2. Calculated as:  number of genomic positions with only one read / number of genomic positions with exactly two reads",
  "Best Species %", "Kraken2", "The percentage of fragments that were classified into the top genome.",
  "Unclassified %", "Kraken2", "The percentage of fragments that were unable to be classified. Often due to short fragment size or the lack of the target genome in the database.",
  "Raw GC%","FASTQC","The mean GC% of raw sequences.",
  "Raw Median Seq Length", "FASTQC", "The median size of the raw sequences",
  "Raw FASTQC fail %", "FASTQC", "The total number of FASTQC metrics that failed. Take this with a grain of salt!",
  "Trimmed GC%","FASTQC","The mean GC% of trimmed sequences.",
  "Trimmed Median Seq Length", "FASTQC", "The median size of the trimmed sequences",
  "Trimmed FASTQC fail %", "FASTQC", "The total number of FASTQC metrics that failed. Take this with a grain of salt!",
  "Trimmed Total Seq","FASTQC","Total number of sequences in the fastq file(s) after trimming."
) |>
  kable(caption = "Definitions for summary stats")

cat("\n</tabber>\n")
```

## Peak Reproducibility

The ATAC and ChIP workflows used ENCODE's reproducibility metrics.  
For the detailed description of these metrics, please click the "Description" tab.

For downstream statistical analyses, it is usually recommended to use the **overlap_conservative** peak set.

<tabber>

```{r, eval=nrow(mod_data$idr_repro) > 0}

cat(
  "|-| IDR =",
  "IDR refers to Irreproducible Discovery Rate and measures consistency between replicates.",
  "Replicates are compared in a pairwise manner and the results from the pooled replicate are transferred.",
  ""
)
kable(mod_data$idr_repro,digits = 3,format.args = list(big.mark = ","))

```

```{r, eval=nrow(mod_data$overlap_repro) > 0}

cat("|-| Overlaps =\n")
kable(mod_data$overlap_repro,digits = 3,format.args = list(big.mark = ","))

```

|-| Description =

The terminology for ENCODE's peak reproducibility is as follows: 

### Calculation Metrics

Nt
: Best no. of peaks passing thresholds by comparing true replicates

Np
: Best no. of peaks passing thresholds by comparing pseudoreplicates

Conservative
: file containing the best peak number when comparing true replicate pairs

Optimal
: peak file with the most peaks when comparing Nt and Np

Rescue Ratio
: max(Nt, Np) / min(Nt, Np)

Consistency Ratio:
: max(Peaks) / min(Peaks)

Peaks are considered passing when both the rescue and consistency ratio are < 2.

Furthermore, ENCODE defines peak sets as either "conservative" or "optimal".

conservative
: Peaks derived from true replicates.

optimal
: Peaks derived from either true or pseudoreplicates.

</tabber>


```{r fastqc, eval=nrow(mod_data$fastqc_raw) > 0 | nrow(mod_data$fastqc_trimmed) > 0}

cat(
  "## FastQC\n",
  "<tabber>\n",
  "",
  sep = "\n"
)

if(nrow(mod_data$fastqc_raw)){
  cat(
    "|-| Raw =\n",
    "Raw represents the report generated by FASTQC prior to any processing of the reads.",
    "",
    sep = "\n"
  )
  kable(mod_data$fastqc_raw,format.args = list(big.mark = ","))
}
if(nrow(mod_data$fastqc_trimmed)){
  cat(
    "|-| Trimmed =\n",
    "Trimmed represents the report generated by FASTQC after adapter trimming.",
    "",
    sep = "\n"
  )
  kable(mod_data$fastqc_trimmed,format.args = list(big.mark = ","))
}

cat("</tabber>\n")

```

```{r fastp, eval=nrow(mod_data$fastp) > 0}

cat(
  "## FASTP",
  "[FASTP](https://github.com/OpenGene/fastp) is a FASTQ preprocessing utility used to remove low quality reads and perform adapter trimming.",
  "",
  sep = "\n\n"
)

mod_data$fastp |>
  kable(format.args = list(big.mark = ","))

```

```{r bowtie2, eval=nrow(mod_data$bowtie2) > 0}

cat("## Bowtie2 Alignments\n\n")

mod_data$bowtie2 |>
  kable(format.args = list(big.mark = ","))

```

```{r tsse, eval=nrow(mod_data$tsse) > 0}

cat(
  "## TSS Enrichment\n",
  "TSS Enrichment (TSSE) is a metric created by ENCODE to estimate the signal to noise ratio surrounding TSS sites.",
  "The calculation is performed by:\n",
  "1. Calculating the depth of 100 bp windows surrounding the TSS. These windows extending to +/- 2,000 bp in either direction.",
  "2. Calculating the depth of the flanking 100 bp windows on either side of this extension and taking the mean.",
  "3. Dividing the depths from step 1 by the mean from step 2.",
  "4. TSSE is represented by the maximum value from step 3.",
  "",
  sep = "\n"
)

mod_data$tsse |>
  kable(digits = 3,caption = "TSS Enrichment Scores", format.args = list(big.mark = ","))

```


```{r homer_fmg, eval=nrow(mod_data$homer_fmg) > 0}

cat(
  "## HOMER Motif Enrichment\n",
  "HOMER was used to scan peak regions for enriched motifs and the top result is presented in the table below.",
  "",
  sep = "\n"
)

mod_data$homer_fmg |>
  mutate(
    across(
      matches("log10 P-value|Q value|n of target seq with motif|n of background seq with motif"),
      as.numeric
    )
  ) |>
  kable(digits = 3,caption = "Top motif hit for each peak set",format.args = list(big.mark = ","))


```
